name: Child Safety Guard
purpose: Defines the non-runtime WellFit child-safety guard for reviewing child/minor-facing language, movement prompts, location concepts, private child data, social exposure, and manipulative retention risks before any implementation is allowed.

connectedSources.relationship: inherits age-appropriate, dignity-preserving, consent-aware, non-manipulative engagement boundaries
inherits pause-friendly, recovery-aware, opt-out-friendly retention boundaries and dark-pattern blocks
inherits protected-data, child/minor, location, consent, data-minimization, and fallback guardrails

reviewAreas.label: altersgerechte Sprache
requiredChecks: Language is understandable for the intended age band without infantilizing or pressuring minors.
Buddy, mission, reward, and social copy avoids shame, fear, guilt, body pressure, medical overclaims, payout hype, and hidden sponsor pressure.
Any age-band assumption remains review_required and must not infer exact age, family role, or vulnerability at runtime.
blockedExamples: Du musst das schaffen, sonst verlierst du deinen Streak.
Teile deine Daten, damit deine Freunde sehen, ob du gut genug bist.

reviewAreas.label: keine gefährlichen Bewegungsaufforderungen
requiredChecks: No missions request traffic-risk, height-risk, water-risk, night-risk, exhaustion, pain, unsafe speed, unsafe load, or unsupervised hazardous movement.
Movement ideas must include safe alternatives, rest language, and adult/guardian/supervisor review where minors or public spaces are involved.
Health, motion, camera, AR, or watch signals must not alone authorize completion, anti-cheat, rewards, or safety clearance.
blockedExamples: Run across streets for bonus points.
Climb a fence or roof for a rare item.
Keep moving despite pain to protect a streak.

reviewAreas.label: keine Standortfreigabe ohne Schutzkonzept
requiredChecks: No exact location sharing, live tracking, meetups, leaderboards, public check-ins, or route disclosure for minors without a separate child-safety, privacy, consent, security, and fallback review plan.
Location concepts must use minimization, short retention, coarse/ephemeral processing where possible, and non-location alternatives.
Permission refusal must not shame, punish, or block safe basic usage where an alternative exists.
blockedExamples: Share your live route with nearby players.
Post a child's school checkpoint publicly.
Require exact GPS to continue a child mission without fallback.

reviewAreas.label: keine privaten Kinderdaten
requiredChecks: No unnecessary collection, display, export, or inference of exact birth date, school, class, address, guardian identity, health details, photos, face data, precise location history, contact details, or family relationships.
Prefer age bands, aggregate status, ephemeral evidence, and least-privilege access where child/minor context is unavoidable after review.
Child/private-data handling remains review_required until legal, privacy, security, and product review define concrete data fields and retention/deletion rules.
blockedExamples: Store a child's school and exact route as default mission history.
Publish child profile photos with leaderboard ranks.
Infer vulnerability or health condition for retention targeting.

reviewAreas.label: keine sozialen Risiken
requiredChecks: No public shaming, direct messaging with unknown users, unmoderated meetups, follower pressure, ranking humiliation, bullying vectors, doxxing risk, or adult-minor discovery surfaces.
Social child/minor concepts require opt-in, guardian/supervision assumptions review, moderation, reporting, blocking, privacy defaults, and safe non-social alternatives.
Social rewards must not pressure minors into disclosure, comparison, purchases, location sharing, or unsafe movement.
blockedExamples: Nearby strangers can invite a child to a challenge.
Public loser boards for missed missions.
Rewards require posting private child progress publicly.

reviewAreas.label: keine manipulative Retention
requiredChecks: No FOMO, loss aversion, scarcity timers, shame streaks, endless loops, sleep-loss pressure, dark-pattern notifications, or child-targeted purchase/payment/token/NFT pressure.
Retention ideas must preserve pause, recovery, opt-out, reminder control, transparent value, and safe alternatives.
Any personalized retention for minors remains blocked until a separate explicit review plan exists.
blockedExamples: Log in now or your Buddy will be sad.
Complete at midnight to avoid losing rare rewards.
Buy/claim token boosts to keep up with friends.

runtimeBlock.statement: Runtime child-safety logic remains blocked until an explicit review plan exists.
minimumReviewPlanRequirements: named product owner and child-safety/privacy/legal/security reviewers
exact files, data fields, UI surfaces, APIs, rules, and tests in scope
data minimization, retention/deletion, access-control, audit, and fallback design
movement safety assessment and prohibited mission taxonomy
social-risk moderation/reporting/blocking plan if any social surface is involved
healthy-retention and ethical-engagement assessment for all engagement loops
rollback plan and explicit statement that client-side rewards/completion are not authoritative

nextSafeMaintenanceTask: Keep the register, architecture document, and validator synchronized; do not implement runtime child-safety logic until a separate explicit review plan is approved.
